def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
        
        Args:
          req_id: request to remove

        Returns:
          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
        """

        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
        if req_index is None:
            return None
        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None

        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)

        # LoRA
        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
        if lora_id != 0:
            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0

        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
        return req_index

    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
        self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
            (i1, i2, MoveDirectionality.SWAP))
        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]

        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp

        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)

        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]

        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)

    def condense(self) -> None:
        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.

        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
        filled.

        Args:
          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.

        Returns:
          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
        """
        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
            return
        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
        if num_reqs == 0:
            # The batched states are empty.
            self._req_ids.clear()
            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
            return

        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
        # is sorted in descending order.
        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
        while empty_req_indices:
            # Find the largest non-empty index.
            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
                last_req_index -= 1

            # Find the smallest empty index.
            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
            assert empty_index is not None
            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
                break

            # Move active request down into empty request
            # index.
            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
                (last_req_index, empty_index,
                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
            assert req_id is not None
            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index

            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
                last_req_index]
            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
            if generator is not None:
                self.generators[empty_index] = generator

            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
                last_req_index]

            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                        last_req_index]

            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
                last_req_index, None)
            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids

            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
            last_req_index -= 1

        # Trim lists to the batch size.
        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]

        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]

3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import Optional

10	DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
11	    temperature=-1.0,
12	    min_p=0.0,
13	    # strictly disabled for now
14	    top_k=0,
15	    top_p=1.0,
16	    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
17	    # presence_penalties=0.0,
18	    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
19	)

22	@dataclass
23	class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
24	    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
25	    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
26	    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
27	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
28	
29	    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
30	    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
31	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
32	
33	    all_greedy: bool = True
34	
35	    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
36	    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
37	    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
38	    logprobs: bool = False
39	
40	    # TODO No penalties for now
41	    no_penalties: bool = True
42	    prompt_token_ids = None
43	    frequency_penalties = None
44	    presence_penalties = None
45	    repetition_penalties = None
46	    # should use tensor
47	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
48	
49	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
50	
51	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
52	        default_factory=lambda: list())
53	
54	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
55	    bad_words_token_ids = None
56	
57	    # Generator not supported by xla
58	    _generators: dict[int,
59	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
60	
61	    @property
62	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
63	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
64	        return self._generators
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def from_input_batch(
68	        cls,
69	        input_batch: InputBatch,
70	        padded_num_reqs: int,
71	        xla_device: torch.device,
72	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.
101	            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val
102	
103	        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
104	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
105	        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
106	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
107	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
108	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
109	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
110	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
111	
112	        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
113	        return cls(
114	            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
115	            to(xla_device),
116	            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
117	            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
118	            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
119	                xla_device),
120	            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
121	                xla_device),
122	            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
123	                xla_device),
124	            logprobs=needs_logprobs)

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch

DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,
    # strictly disabled for now
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
    # presence_penalties=0.0,
    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
)


@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None

    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None

    all_greedy: bool = True

    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
    logprobs: bool = False

    # TODO No penalties for now
    no_penalties: bool = True
    prompt_token_ids = None
    frequency_penalties = None
    presence_penalties = None
    repetition_penalties = None
    # should use tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())

    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized

    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: list())

    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
    bad_words_token_ids = None

    # Generator not supported by xla
    _generators: dict[int,
                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())

    @property
    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
        return self._generators

    @classmethod
    def from_input_batch(
        cls,
        input_batch: InputBatch,
        padded_num_reqs: int,
        xla_device: torch.device,
        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
        """
        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.

        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.

        Args:
            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
            xla_device: The XLA device.
            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
        """
        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)

        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs

        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
            # Pad value is the default one.
            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val

        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])

        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
        return cls(
            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
            to(xla_device),
            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            logprobs=needs_logprobs)

###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here


class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.min_p_count: int = 0

        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        # Pre-allocated device tensor
        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        # Current slice of the device tensor
        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        # Process added requests.
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
                needs_update = True
                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
            if min_p:
                self.min_p_count += 1

        if self.min_p_count:
            # Process removed requests.
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
                    self.min_p_count -= 1

            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (min_p_a :=
                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b

        # Update tensors if needed.
        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits


class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
        super().__init__()
        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
        self.device = device
        self.pin_memory = pin_memory

        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
        return False

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        # Process added requests.
        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
                                                       params.logit_bias):
                self.biases[index] = lb
            else:
                self.biases.pop(index, None)

        if self.biases:

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	import dataclasses
4	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
5	from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence
6	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
7	from enum import Enum
8	from itertools import chain
9	from typing import Optional, Union
10	
11	import torch
12	from torch._prims_common import DeviceLikeType
13	
14	from vllm import PoolingParams, SamplingParams
15	from vllm.logger import init_logger
16	
17	logger = init_logger(__name__)
18	
19	
20	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
21	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
22	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
23	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
24	    SWAP = 1
25	
26	
27	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
28	# requests added to the batch.
29	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
30	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
31	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
32	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
33	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
34	RemovedRequest = int
35	
36	
37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
54	
55	
56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and

file.content:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
import dataclasses
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from itertools import chain
from typing import Optional, Union

import torch
from torch._prims_common import DeviceLikeType

from vllm import PoolingParams, SamplingParams
from vllm.logger import init_logger

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
    SWAP = 1


# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
# requests added to the batch.
AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
# Batch indices of any removed requests.
RemovedRequest = int


@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class BatchUpdate:
    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch

    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
    # within the persistent batch.
    #
    # Note: each added request is represented as
    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
    # generated tokens
    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]


class BatchUpdateBuilder:
    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
    
    Assumptions:
    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
      are registered using self.removed_append()
    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
      self.pop_removed())
    
    Guarantees under above assumptions:
    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
      the lowest removed request index in the current step
    """

    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
    _is_removed_sorted: bool
    moved: list[MovedRequest]
    added: list[AddedRequest]

    def __init__(
        self,
        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._removed = removed or []
        self.moved = moved or []
        self.added = added or []
        self._is_removed_sorted = False

    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
        """Sort removed request indices in
        descending order.
        
        Idempotent after first call in a
        given step, until reset.
        """
        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
            self._is_removed_sorted = True

    @property
    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
        """Removed request indices sorted in
        descending order"""
        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
        return self._removed

    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
        """Register the removal of a request from
        the persistent batch.

        Must not be called after the first time
        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
        
        Args:
          index: request index
        """
        if self._is_removed_sorted:
            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
                               " self.removed has been read.")
        self._removed.append(index)

    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
        return bool(self._removed)

    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
        """Return lowest removed request index"""
        if self.has_removed():
            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
            return self._removed[-1]
        return None

    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
        if self.has_removed():
            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
            return self._removed.pop()
        return None

    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
        and reset internal batch update builder state.
        
        Args:
          batch_size: current persistent batch size

        Returns:
          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
        """
        # Reset removal-sorting logic
        self._is_removed_sorted = False
        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
            # No update; short-circuit
            return None
        # Build batch state update
        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
            batch_size=batch_size,
            removed=self._removed,
            moved=self.moved,
            added=self.added,
        )
        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
        self._removed = []
        self.moved = []
        self.added = []
        return batch_update


class LogitsProcessor(ABC):

    @abstractmethod
    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        raise NotImplementedError

    @abstractmethod
    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """True if logits processor has no impact on the
        argmax computation in greedy sampling.
        NOTE: may or may not have the same value for all
        instances of a given LogitsProcessor subclass,
        depending on subclass implementation.
        TODO(andy): won't be utilized until logits
        processors are user-extensible
        """
        raise NotImplementedError

    @abstractmethod
    def update_state(
        self,
        batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate],
    ) -> None:
        """Called when there are new output tokens, prior
        to each forward pass.

        Args:
            batch_update is non-None iff there have been
            changes to the batch makeup.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError


@dataclass
class LogitsProcessorManager:
    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""
    argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
        default_factory=list)  # argmax-invariant logitsprocs
    non_argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs

    @property
    def all(self) -> Iterator[LogitsProcessor]:
        """Iterator over all logits processors."""
        return chain(self.argmax_invariant, self.non_argmax_invariant)

input.prompt:
I need to design a plan for adding "Top-A" sampling to vLLM. Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability >= `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. When top_a == 0.0, it's a no-op.

## Key insight: top_a is algorithmically identical to min_p

Looking at the existing `_apply_min_p` in V0 (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py lines 427-441):
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits, min_p):
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```
And in V1's MinPLogitsProcessor (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py lines 290-306):
```python
def apply(self, logits):
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
    invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
    logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
    return logits
```

**Top-A filtering (`p(token) < top_a * max_p → mask out`) is mathematically the same operation as min_p filtering.** The only difference is naming and the range convention: min_p is [0,1] (0=disabled), top_a is [0,1] (0=disabled).

## Codebase architecture summary

### Two major paths exist:

**V0 path (legacy):**
1. `SamplingParams` → `SamplingTensors` (via `from_sampling_metadata`) → sampler functions
2. Files: `vllm/sampling_params.py`, `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`
3. min_p handled via `_apply_min_p()` standalone function and `do_min_p` flag

**V1 path (GPU):**
1. `SamplingParams` → `InputBatch` (gpu_input_batch.py) → `SamplingMetadata` → `Sampler`
2. min_p is NOT in gpu_input_batch.py directly — instead it's a `MinPLogitsProcessor` in `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` that's registered as argmax-invariant
3. min_p gets its own tensor management within the logits processor

**V1 path (TPU):**
1. `SamplingParams` → `InputBatch` (tpu_input_batch.py) → `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` → TPU sampler
2. min_p is in tpu_input_batch.py directly
3. TPU sampler has its own `apply_min_p` method

**OpenAI API protocol:**
- `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` — 3 request classes with top_p/top_k fields

## Files that need modification

1. **`vllm/sampling_params.py`** — Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field, docstring, validation, greedy reset, `from_optional`, `__repr__`

2. **V0 path:**
   - **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** — Add `top_as` to `SamplingTensors`, extraction in `from_sampling_metadata`, `from_lists`; add `do_top_a` flag
   - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** — Add `_apply_top_a()` function (same logic as `_apply_min_p`), wire into `forward()` with `do_top_a` flag

3. **V1 GPU path:**
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`** — Add `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modeled on `MinPLogitsProcessor`), register it in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` as argmax-invariant

4. **V1 TPU path:**
   - **`vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`** — Add `top_a` CPU/GPU tensors and tracking (mirroring min_p pattern)
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`** — Add `top_a` to `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` and `DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`** — Add `apply_top_a` method (same as `apply_min_p`)

5. **OpenAI API:**
   - **`vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** — Add `top_a` field to the 3 request classes, add to `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`, thread through `to_sampling_params`

## Design decisions

1. **Naming**: `top_a` as field name, consistent with `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`
2. **Default**: 0.0 (disabled, same convention as min_p)
3. **Validation**: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0`
4. **Greedy reset**: Reset to 0.0 when temperature < eps (same as min_p)
5. **V1 GPU implementation**: As a `LogitsProcessor` (following min_p pattern) — this is cleaner and avoids modifying the top_k/top_p tensor pipeline or the FlashInfer path
6. **V0 implementation**: As a standalone `_apply_top_a()` function (following min_p pattern)
7. **Ordering**: Apply after top_k/top_p but interchangeable with min_p since both are probability-threshold filters

Please produce a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with specific file paths, line numbers, and code changes needed.

2. **V0 path:**

   - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** — Add `_apply_top_a()` function (same logic as `_apply_min_p`), wire into `forward()` with `do_top_a` flag

3. **V1 GPU path:**
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`** — Add `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modeled on `MinPLogitsProcessor`), register it in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` as argmax-invariant

4. **V1 TPU path:**
   - **`vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`** — Add `top_a` CPU/GPU tensors and tracking (mirroring min_p pattern)
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`** — Add `top_a` to `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` and `DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`
   - **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`** — Add `apply_top_a` method (same as `apply_min_p`)

5. **OpenAI API:**
   - **`vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** — Add `top_a` field to the 3 request classes, add to `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`, thread through `to_sampling_params`

## Design decisions

1. **Naming**: `top_a` as field name, consistent with `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`
2. **Default**: 0.0 (disabled, same convention as min_p)
3. **Validation**: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0`
4. **Greedy reset**: Reset to 0.0 when temperature < eps (same as min_p)
5. **V1 GPU implementation**: As a `LogitsProcessor` (following min_p pattern) — this is cleaner and avoids modifying the top_k/top_p tensor pipeline or the FlashInfer path
6. **V0 implementation**: As a standalone `_apply_top_a()` function (following min_p pattern)
7. **Ordering**: Apply after top_k/top_p but interchangeable with min_p since both are probability-threshold filters

Please produce a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with specific file paths, line numbers, and code changes needed.

118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1

327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,

343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:

387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:

391	        if self.n < 1:

397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "

410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:

414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:

426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:

436	            raise ValueError(

464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:

483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
550	
551	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
553	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
554	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
555	        """
556	
557	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
562	
563	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
566	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
567	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
568	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
569	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
570	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
573	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
574	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
575	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
576	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
577	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
578	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
579	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
580	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
581	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
582	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
583	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
584	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
585	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
586	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
587	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
588	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
589	
590	
591	class BeamSearchParams(
592	        msgspec.Struct,
593	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
594	        # required for @cached_property.
595	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
596	    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
597	    beam_width: int
598	    max_tokens: int
599	    ignore_eos: bool = False
600	    temperature: float = 0.0
601	    length_penalty: float = 1.0
602	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False

4	from array import array
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Optional
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams, SamplingType
11	from vllm.sequence import (VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE, SequenceData,
12	                           SequenceGroupMetadata)
13	from vllm.utils import (PyObjectCache, async_tensor_h2d,
14	                        is_pin_memory_available, make_tensor_with_pad)
15	
16	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]
51	
52	    @property
53	    def do_sample(self):
54	        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
55	
56	    def __post_init__(self):
57	        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
58	            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
59	        if self.is_prompt:
60	            assert self.seq_len is not None
61	            assert self.query_len is not None
62	
63	
64	def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
65	    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
66	        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
67	        sampling_params=None,
68	        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
69	        seq_len=0,
70	        query_len=0,
71	        generator=None,
72	        is_prompt=True,
73	        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
74	        sample_indices=[],
75	    )
76	
77	
78	class SamplingMetadataCache:
79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""
80	
81	    def __init__(self):
82	        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}
83	
84	    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
87	                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))
88	
89	        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
90	        return obj
91	
92	    def reset(self):
93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
94	            cache.reset()
95	
96	
97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")
196	
197	
198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
348	            sample_obj.generator = generator
349	            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
350	        else:
351	            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
352	                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
353	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
354	                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
355	                seq_len=seq_len,
356	                query_len=query_len,
357	                generator=generator,
358	                is_prompt=is_prompt,
359	                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
360	                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
361	            )
362	
363	        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)
364	
365	    if cache is not None:
366	        cache.reset()
367	
368	    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
369	            num_prompts)
370	
371	
372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496	
497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
506	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
507	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
508	        output_tokens: list[array],
509	        vocab_size: int,
510	        device: torch.device,
511	        dtype: torch.dtype,
512	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
513	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
514	        # pinned memory.
515	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:
520	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
521	                prompt_tokens,
522	                vocab_size,
523	                device="cpu",
524	                dtype=torch.int64,
525	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
526	            )
527	            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
528	                output_tokens,
529	                vocab_size,
530	                device="cpu",
531	                dtype=torch.int64,
532	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
533	            )
534	        else:
535	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
536	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
537	            output_t = empty_tensor
538	
539	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
540	            temperatures,
541	            device="cpu",
542	            dtype=dtype,
543	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	        )
545	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
546	            top_ps,
547	            device="cpu",
548	            dtype=dtype,
549	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
550	        )
551	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
552	            min_ps,
553	            device="cpu",
554	            dtype=dtype,
555	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
556	        )
557	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
558	            presence_penalties,
559	            device="cpu",
560	            dtype=dtype,
561	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
562	        )
563	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
564	            frequency_penalties,
565	            device="cpu",
566	            dtype=dtype,
567	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
568	        )
569	        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
570	            repetition_penalties,
571	            device="cpu",
572	            dtype=dtype,
573	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
574	        )
575	        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
576	            top_ks,
577	            device="cpu",
578	            dtype=torch.int,
579	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
580	        )
581	        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
582	        # transfer to device.
583	
584	        return cls(
585	            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
586	            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
587	            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
588	            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
589	            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
590	                                                       non_blocking=True),
591	            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
592	                                                         non_blocking=True),
593	            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
594	                                                           non_blocking=True),
595	            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
596	            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
597	        )

20	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
21	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
22	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
23	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
24	    SWAP = 1
25	
26	
27	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
28	# requests added to the batch.
29	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
30	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
31	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
32	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
33	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
34	RemovedRequest = int
